business.headline: Planning to buy a plot in Noida? Read this Noida Authority warning firstThe Noida Authority has warned people against buying or selling land under specified khasra numbers in three villages. It said the parcels are acquired or notified land where illegal construction could bring financial and legal trouble.

Missed your EMI by a day? Here's how it can affect your credit scoreDid you miss your EMI by a day and instantly worry about your CIBIL score? Before you panic, here's what actually happens behind the scenes.

AI mega deal: Why HCLTech shares jump nearly 6% today?At around 11 am, HCLTech was trading nearly 6% higher. The rally also helped lift the broader Nifty IT index for the second straight session, even as AI-heavy markets in South Korea and Taiwan witnessed a sharp selloff.

Air India cuts fuel surcharge on North America, Europe flights as oil easesAir India has reduced temporary fuel surcharges on select long-haul routes from July 1. The move follows easing oil prices after the West Asia conflict had pushed up operating costs.
Sensex opens 400 points higher, Nifty above 24,300; HCLTech up 4%The BSE Sensex rose 507 points to 78,009.37, while the NSE Nifty50 gained nearly 170 points to trade at 24,346.25 in early trading.

Is the EPFO portal working today? Here's what PF subscribers should knowEPFO has said member and employer services are expected to resume from July 3 after another maintenance extension. The prolonged upgrade has delayed PF claims, passbook access and compliance tasks for nearly a week.

Zero tax in Form 16? You may still have to file an ITR. Here's whyEven if your employer has deducted no income tax because you qualify for the rebate under Section 87A, you may still be legally required to file an ITR in several situations.

EPF Scheme 2026: What employees and employers need to knowWith around eight crore active EPFO subscribers, the government hopes the updated scheme will make provident fund management simpler, more efficient and better suited to a digital-first workplace.

Will petrol, diesel prices be cut? Hardeep Puri says here's when it's possiblePuri explained that although international crude oil prices have fallen in recent weeks, oil companies are still refining crude that was purchased when prices were much higher during the West Asia conflict.

Centre eyes LIC, PSU stake sales to cushion strain from oil crisis: ReportThe Centre is speeding up stake sales in LIC, Hindustan Zinc and other state-run firms. The move is aimed at creating fiscal room as oil price volatility pressures government finances.

Sensex jumps 579 points as IT stocks shine; Nifty closes above 24,175The BSE Sensex climbed 579.48 points, or 0.75%, to close at 77,502.12, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 169.85 points, or 0.71%, to settle at 24,175.70.

AI rally cracks: Why Asian markets are crashing while Indian IT stocks rallyAsian chip stocks slumped after reports stoked fears of excess AI computing capacity. Indian IT shares, more tied to software spending, rose as investors weighed a different AI demand story.

Want to save more tax under new regime? Ask your employer about this NPS benefitWith ITR filing in full swing, our 'KNOW YOUR TAX' series helps you understand the tax benefits that still matter. This edition explains how an employer's NPS contribution can help salaried employees save tax while building a retirement corpus.
Mehli Mistry steps down from Ratan Tata's family officeMistry's resignation from RNT Associates comes months after he was removed from Tata Trusts in November 2025.

EPFO new rule: PF contribution above Rs 1,800 now voluntary; what it meansThe updated rules are expected to make the EPF system simpler, more digital and easier to use for nearly 8 crore active EPFO subscribers. The new scheme came into effect on June 29 after it was published in the Gazette, replacing the long-standing EPF Scheme, 1952.

Why are IT stocks rising today? Infosys, HCLTech, TCS jump up to 5%Infosys, HCLTech, TCS rise: IT stocks rebounded sharply on Thursday, led by Infosys and other large-cap names. The recovery reflected a shift from overheated AI chip plays to software firms seen as steadier AI beneficiaries.

Rs 35,000-crore investment push linked to Maruti plant during Japan PM's visitThe Kharkhoda plant is Maruti Suzuki's fourth manufacturing facility in India and is expected to significantly boost the country's passenger vehicle production capacity.

Sensex jumps nearly 400 points, Nifty tops 24,100 as IT stocks reboundIT stocks led the rally, while falling crude oil prices and easing geopolitical concerns boosted investor confidence. However, a weak monsoon remains a key concern.

OTT subscriptions becoming a trap? 8 in 10 users say they're being manipulatedA nationwide survey has found that eight in ten OTT users in India say they have encountered 'dark patterns', deceptive design practices that nudge users into spending more, continuing subscriptions or making choices they may not have intended.

Energy crisis: Is the worst now over? Sajjid Chinoy respondsPrices of commercial LPG were cut by Rs 183.50 per 19-kg cylinder on Wednesday – the first reduction this year following a series of hikes in the wake of the West Asia crisis that caused energy supply disruptions across the globe.

entertainment.headline: Alpha has no post-credit scene: Spy universe breaks its most exciting tradition Alpha is now in theatres. Find out whether the Alia Bhatt and Sharvari-led YRF Spy Universe film features a post-credit scene, plus everything to know about its cast and story.

Gatta Kusthi 2 review: Men talk feminism in Vishnu Vishal film, but women pay for itRelease Date:Fri Jul 03, 2026 Gatta Kusthi 2 review: Director Chella Ayyavu's Gatta Kusthi 2, starring Vishnu Vishal and Aishwarya Lekshmi, is a family entertainer with a wrestling backdrop. The film's lopsided take on gender politics and comedy that never lands make it a sequel that could have been done away with.

Vithabai's daughter backs Eetha title amid row over Shraddha Kapoor film The title of the upcoming film Eetha, based on legendary lavani artiste Vithabai Narayangaonkar, had sparked debate but her eldest daughter has now expressed support for it. She believes the title is fitting and welcomes the film as a tribute.

Pritam and Pedro review: A refreshing break from dark, high-stakes thrillersRelease Date:Fri Jul 03, 2026 Pritam and Pedro series review: The series, featuring Arshad Warsi and Vir Hirani, pairs a cyber-savvy young man with an old-school officer on a kidnapping case. The JioHotstar series is a light-hearted buddy-cop drama set in Goa, blending simple cyber crime with humour and heartfelt moments.

Alpha X review: Alia Bhatt and Sharvari impress, but the story divides audiences Alpha X review: The first audience verdict on Alia Bhatt and Sharvari's spy thriller is out. See why the film is generating buzz across social media.

Did Taylor Swift invite ex-boyfriend Harry Styles to her wedding? What we know Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce reportedly invited Swift's ex-boyfriend, Harry Styles, to their New York wedding celebrations. The duo dated between 2012 and early 2013.

Alpha vs Dhurandhar: The battle for India's new spy benchmark Ranveer Singh's Dhurandhar redefined Indian spy cinema with gritty realism. Can Alia Bhatt and Sharvari's Alpha evolve the YRF Spy Universe beyond spectacle?

Did Hrithik Roshan pay Rs 400 crore in alimony to Sussanne? Her sister breaks silence Jewellery designer Farah Khan Ali has addressed reports that her sister, Sussanne Khan, sought or received Rs 400 crore in alimony from Hrithik Roshan. She said the divorce was amicable and that the former couple continue to share a warm bond with both families.

Secret wedding wasn't all, Taylor Swift-Travis Kelce donate $26 million to charity Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce have donated $26 million to 20 charities ahead of their grand wedding celebrations at New York's Madison Square Garden on Friday. However, reports also claim that the couple have married in a private ceremony in Nashville, Swift's hometown.

Jailed Anmol Bishnoi seeks to surrender before court in Salman Khan firing case Anmol Bishnoi, the brother of jailed gangster Lawrence Bishnoi, has moved a special MCOCA court in Mumbai seeking permission to surrender in the Salman Khan house firing case, saying he wishes to voluntarily join the judicial proceedings "in the interest of a fair trial and justice."

Baby Do Die Do review: Huma Qureshi powers this pulpy revenge thriller with styleRelease Date:Fri Jul 03, 2026 Baby Do Die Do review: Baby Karmarkar, a deaf and mute contract killer, hunts her sister's murderers in a pulpy revenge saga. Huma Qureshi anchors the film as it blends old-school Bollywood excess with confident storytelling.

Aamir Khan seeks blessings ahead of July 5 wedding with Gauri Spratt Addressing the media ahead of his wedding, Aamir Khan confirmed that he will marry Gauri Spratt on July 5 in an intimate ceremony at his Mumbai home. The actor said only close family and friends will attend the celebration .

Jasmin Bhasin discharged from Dubai hospital, shares health update Jasmin Bhasin says she is back home in India after being discharged from a Dubai hospital. Her latest update says there is no danger as she rests and recovers after the health scare.

Are Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce already married? Report says they are The pair exchanged vows in front of a 'tiny group of loved ones,' the New York Post's Page Six reported. The outlet did not say when or where the nuptials took place, though it noted Swift's private jet recently spent time in Nashville.

Lock Upp 2: Vinny Arora calls Shreya Kalra a loser for targeting husband Dheeraj Actor Vinny Arora has hit back at social media influencer Shreya Kalra after her on-screen clash with Dheeraj Dhoopar in Lock Upp: Sach Ya Sazaa. Vinny accused Shreya for her "cheap, revenge-seeking" strategy against Dheeraj.

SS Rajamouli joins Martin Scorsese, Clint Eastwood on France's Wall of Filmmakers Director SS Rajamouli was honoured at Institut Lumière in Lyon with a plaque on its Wall of Filmmakers. The recognition places him alongside Hollywood icons like Martin Scorsese and Clint Eastwood and adds to the global reach of his work.

Vicky Kaushal's three-word monsoon post for Katrina Kaif is pure romance Actor Vicky Kaushal shared a rain-soaked black-and-white photograph of wife Katrina Kaif hugging him from behind. The post drew fan reactions and spotlighted the couple's life after welcoming their son Vihaan.

Homebound cinematographer Pratik Shah apologises over sexual misconduct allegations Homebound cinematographer Pratik Shah has apologised for online allegations of sexual misconduct and emotional abuse made last year. His statement follows months of fallout, including reported removals from projects and final credits.

Jailer 2 release date announced: Rajinikanth's action sequel to arrive in October Rajinikanth's Jailer 2 finally has a release date. Sun Pictures has unveiled when the much-awaited sequel will arrive in cinemas, along with fresh details about the returning team and expanded cast.

general news.headline: SC says Sonam Raghuvanshi will remain on bail as she's already outThe Supreme Court said that allegations against Sonam Raghuvanshi were "quite serious" and agreed to examine the Meghalaya Police's challenge to the bail order in detail on Thursday. However, after being informed that Sonam had already been released, the bench said it did not wish to interfere at this stage.

Military big part of Pak's problem, not India: Ex-Singapore envoy's blunt takeIn an unsparing assessment of Pakistan, former Singapore envoy Bilahari Kausikan said the diplomatic gains by Pakistan will do little to address the country's economic crisis. He also said Pakistan's problem is not India, but its politicians and the military.

Were Croatia robbed? Why VAR disallowed dramatic equaliser vs PortugalFIFA World Cup: Croatia's campaign ended in agony after a dramatic stoppage-time equaliser against Portugal was ruled out following a VAR review in their Round of 32 clash on Thursday.

US warned Iran of possible Israeli plot to kill top ceasefire negotiatorsThe US reportedly asked Middle Eastern countries to warn Iran about possible Israeli assassination attempts targeting senior officials, fearing such a move could derail ceasefire talks that got underway in early April.

Did we get Haldighati winner wrong for 450 years?It is commonly accepted that Akbar won the Battle of Haldighati. But did Maharana Pratap win the war? A fresh reading of the 450-year-old battle amid US President Donald Trump's repeated claims that the US won the war against Iran.

Woman, 26, checks into Hyderabad hotel with man, found dead next morningA 26-year-old woman was found dead in a Hyderabad hotel room after checking in with a man. Her family has alleged murder, while police are examining all angles.

2 apps used to turn off e-rickshaws taken down after prank videos go viralViral videos showed people using smartphone apps to remotely switch off moving e-rickshaws, triggering safety concerns and prompting the Centre to remove two such apps from app stores.

Video: LPG tanker erupts into fireball after crash at UP toll plaza; 4 deadAn LPG tanker travelling from Kanpur to Varanasi crashed into a divider and caught fire at a toll plaza in Uttar Pradesh's Kaushambi district. CCTV footage of the incident surfaced as the death toll rose to four while two toll workers remain hospitalised.

Live: Fans say Kabir's action is better than HamzaAlpha Movie Release and Review Live: The film starring Alia Bhatt, Sharvari, Bobby Deol and Anil Kapoor, has hit theatres, and fans are already flooding social media with their first reactions. While Alia and Sharvari's action-packed performances are winning praise, Bobby Deol's intense act is also earning applause.

40 killed, 8 injured after bus falls into roadside ditch in Pakistan's Balochistan40 people were killed and eight others injured after a bus fell into a deep roadside ditch in Pakistan's southwestern province of Balochistan.
Mumbai rain: Satellite captures humongous cloud build-up over MaharashtraSatellite images showed a massive rain-bearing cloud mass advancing from the Arabian Sea towards Maharashtra on Friday.

Founder's moving post for son who traded corporate comfort for deep-sea divingMaheshwer Peri, founder and chairman of education platform Careers360, said his son Vinayak studied engineering and later worked in product, where he was good at his job, well-liked and was adding value. From a parent's perspective, everything about the role seemed right.

Bengaluru Police makes first arrest in Capgemini daycare toddler abuse caseViral videos showed toddlers being forced into a washing machine drum, sprayed with water, and locked in bathrooms at Capgemini's on-campus daycare centre in Bengaluru, triggering massive outrage and police action.

Goodbye 21 points: India racing ahead with badminton's new scoring systemThe Badminton Association of India has proactively fast-tracked the BWF's revolutionary 3x15 scoring system into its domestic circuit from July 2026, ensuring Indian shuttlers master the high-intensity format well before its 2027 global rollout.

As a Mumbaikar, I am glad I am away from Bambai ki baarish this yearThe romance of Bambai ki baarish fades quickly when every spell of rain brings deaths, flooded roads, and misery in forms Mumbaikars have accepted to live with. A Mumbaikar explains why he's relieved to be away from Mumbai this monsoon.

Alpha vs Dhurandhar: The battle for India's new spy benchmarkRanveer Singh's Dhurandhar redefined Indian spy cinema with gritty realism. Can Alia Bhatt and Sharvari's Alpha evolve the YRF Spy Universe beyond spectacle?

Venezuela quake miracle: Man buried for 8 days rescued after 100-hour missionAfter being trapped beneath a collapsed shopping centre for eight days, security guard Hernan Alberto Gil Flores was pulled out alive by rescuers, offering a rare glimmer of hope amid Venezuela's earthquake devastation and growing criticism of the government's response.

Can't exile citizens for anti-govt protest: High Court junks Mumbai Police orderThe Bombay High Court scrapped a Mumbai Police order that barred a political activist from entering the city. The court held that the activist's protests against the government could not justify such a restriction.

Baby Do Die Do review: Huma Qureshi powers this pulpy revenge thriller with styleRelease Date:Fri Jul 03, 2026Baby Do Die Do review: Baby Karmarkar, a deaf and mute contract killer, hunts her sister's murderers in a pulpy revenge saga. Huma Qureshi anchors the film as it blends old-school Bollywood excess with confident storytelling.

Dubai's 4-day work week got us thinking: What would Indians do with an extra leave?This summer, Dubai government employees get to enjoy three-day weekends. But what if Indians got it too? How would they spend that extra day? We asked around.

From Nashik to Pune: Maharashtra sweeps top 3 ranks in CA Foundation 2026 Live UpdatesThe ICAI CA Foundation May 2026 results are out, and this year's top three ranks have all gone to students from Maharashtra. Here's a look at the toppers and the latest pass percentage.

Ujjain: Men hack e-rickshaws with Bluetooth app, then charge drivers to fix themPolice in Ujjain have detained an 18-year-old suspect in a case involving e-rickshaws allegedly disrupted through a mobile app. The probe now centres on technical evidence, CCTV footage and warnings to drivers against paying unknown helpers.

Vijay formed govt through defections. Now TVK accuses rivals of poaching MLAsAfter forming its government with the support of defectors, Vijay's party has stirred political circles in Tamil Nadu by claiming a bid to topple its barely two-month-old government. The developments show the fragility of the political arithmetic in Tamil Nadu.

sports.headline: FIFA World Cup: Cristiano Ronaldo, Portugal take over the streets of Toronto Portugal's dramatic FIFA World Cup Round of 32 victory over Croatia sparked jubilant scenes in Toronto on Thursday night, with hundreds of supporters gathering outside the team's hotel. The Portugal team also joined in, leading the chants from their team hotel.

Mahrez's final World Cup campaign cut short as clinical Switzerland reach R16 Switzerland made it into their fourth straight Round of 16 in the FIFA World Cup. The clinical Swiss beat Algeria 2-0 in Vancouver, cutting short Riyad Mahrez's final World Cup campaign.

Nagelsmann quits Germany job after World Cup debacle, Klopp favourite to take over Julian Nagelsmann has stepped down as Germany's head coach after the four-time world champions crashed out of the FIFA World Cup 2026 in the Round of 32, ending his nearly three-year tenure in charge.

Goodbye 21 points: India racing ahead with badminton's new scoring system The Badminton Association of India has proactively fast-tracked the BWF's revolutionary 3x15 scoring system into its domestic circuit from July 2026, ensuring Indian shuttlers master the high-intensity format well before its 2027 global rollout.

Wimbledon Day 5 order of play: Djokovic, Osaka headline third-round action The third round of Wimbledon begins on Friday, July 3, with Novak Djokovic, Naomi Osaka and Aryna Sabalenka leading the day's action, while defending champion Jannik Sinner is set for a surprise switch from Centre Court to No. 1 Court for his third-round clash.

Were Croatia robbed? Why VAR disallowed dramatic equaliser vs Portugal FIFA World Cup: Croatia's campaign ended in agony after a dramatic stoppage-time equaliser against Portugal was ruled out following a VAR review in their Round of 32 clash on Thursday.

Ronaldo consoles Modric as Portugal knock Croatia out in late VAR drama FIFA World Cup: Cristiano Ronaldo consoled a tearful Luka Modric after Portugal edged Croatia 2-1 in a dramatic Round of 32 clash on Thursday, ending the Croatian captain's campaign in heartbreaking fashion.

SUI 2-0 ALG at Full Time: Embolo, Ndoye score as the Swiss reach R16 Switzerland (SUI) vs Algeria (ALG) Highlights, FIFA World Cup 2026: A clinical Switzerland team go through to the Round of 16 with a 2-0 win over Algeria. Embolo and Ndoye score in either half as the Swiss march on to the next knockout round of the World Cup.

Cristiano Ronaldo's World Cup dream lives on after Portugal survive Croatia FIFA World Cup 2026: Portugal came from behind to beat Croatia 2-1 in Toronto, with Cristiano Ronaldo scoring from the spot and Goncalo Ramos heading a stoppage-time winner. The result sends Portugal into a Round of 16 clash with Spain and leaves Roberto Martinez weighing a major selection call.

Ronaldo in tears wearing No. 21 as Portugal honour Jota with epic win vs Croatia Cristiano Ronaldo wore Diogo Jota's No. 21 shirt and left the pitch in tears after Portugal's dramatic 2-1 win over Croatia on Thursday, as the national team paid an emotional tribute to their late teammate on the first anniversary of his death.

Pints till penalties! PM Starmer lets pubs stay open for England vs Mexico FIFA World Cup 2026: The UK government has allowed pubs and bars in England and Wales to stay open until 5 a.m. for England's World Cup Round of 16 match against Mexico. The move cuts red tape for venues and gives the hospitality trade a timely lift for a high-stakes late-night fixture.

POR 2-1 CRO Highlights: Portugal are through to the last 16 after late VAR dramaPortugal (POR) vs Croatia (CRO) Highlights, FIFA World Cup 2026: (POR 2-1 CRO) Cristiano Ronaldo scored the first World Cupknockout-stage goal of his career, Goncalo Ramos netted a stoppage-time header and Portugal got a last-second break on an offside call to beat Croatia 2-1 on Thursday and set up a last-16 clash with Spain.

Spain too good for Austria, advance to last 16 with easeFIFA World Cup 2026: Spain cruised into the Round of 16 with a dominant 3-0 victory over Austria, as Mikel Oyarzabal scored twice and Pedro Porro added his first international goal. Luis de la Fuente's side controlled the contest from start to finish and will now face either Portugal or Croatia in the next round.

England Women bury South Africa ghosts, set up blockbuster final vs AustraliaWomen's T20 World Cup, ENGW vs SAW: England beat South Africa by 40 runs and set up a final with Australia on Sunday at the iconic Lord's Cricket Ground in London.

Elena Rybakina sends World No.1 warning, but refuses to get carried awayElena Rybakina played down the World No. 1 race, saying her focus is solely on improving and winning matches at Wimbledon. The Australian Open champion could overtake Aryna Sabalenka with a deep run at the All England Club.

Alexander Zverev, Elena Rybakina ease into Round 3 with clinical displaysFrench Open champion Alexander Zverev and former Wimbledon winner Elena Rybakina sent out strong title statements with dominant second-round wins. Both stars advanced in straight sets, showcasing the form that makes them leading contenders at the All England Club.

Iga Swiatek braces for tricky grass test against high-flying Alexandra EalaDefending champion Iga Swiatek is expecting a stern third-round test against Alexandra Eala at Wimbledon, with their head-to-head locked at 1-1 and the Pole describing the Filipina's grass-court game as tricky.

ESP 3–0 AUT Highlights: Spain too hot to handle for AustriaSpain (ESP) vs Austria (AUT) Highlights, FIFA World Cup: (ESP 3–0 AUT) Spain underlined their title credentials with a dominant 3-0 win over Austria, powered by Mikel Oyarzabal's brace and Pedro Porro's maiden international goal. Luis de la Fuente's side barely looked troubled as they booked their place in the World Cup Round of 16 in emphatic fashion.

Can Lewis Hamilton stop Mercedes? His cheeky answer Is going viralLewis Hamilton joked that only loosening Mercedes bolts would make him a title threat before Silverstone. The remark exposed Ferrari's gap to the benchmark as Kimi Antonelli's rookie campaign continues to dictate the season.

ENG vs SA semifinals Highlights: England qualify for final, beat South Africa by 40 runsEngland (ENG-W) vs South Africa (SA-W) semifinals Highlights, Women's T20 World Cup: England have stormed to the final of the tournament after beating South Africa by 40 runs at the Oval. They will now face Australia in the final on July 5.

Turkey's Zeynep Sonmez steals spotlight with unique symbol on racketTurkish player Zeynep Sonmez used a watermelon-shaped racket dampener at Wimbledon 2026 after reportedly being barred from wearing a Palestine-support brooch, saying the fruit symbol allowed her to express solidarity with Palestinians.

India's football capital, Kolkata dresses up for World Cup knockoutsFIFA World Cup 2026: A neighbourhood club in Kolkata has turned a Shyambazar lane into a World Cup-themed FIFA Gali with murals, flags and giant cutouts of football stars. The display captures the city's enduring football obsession and its hope that India will one day reach the FIFA World Cup.

Jai Moondra rewarded for heroics vs India with European T20 League berthIreland pacer Jai Moondra was rewarded for his match-winning performances against India by becoming Rotterdam Dockers' first pick in the inaugural European T20 Premier League Draft.

Iga Swiatek cruises through to Round 3, Fritz, De Minaur maintain perfect startsDefending champion Iga Swiatek stormed into the Wimbledon third round with a straight-sets win over Karolina Pliskova, while Taylor Fritz and Alex de Minaur also advanced with commanding victories.

Samit Dravid forges own path in Maharaja Trophy as fans see shades of Rahul DravidSamit Dravid continues to impress in the Maharaja Trophy KSCA T20 2026, with fans drawing comparisons to father Rahul Dravid as the youngster showcases his own promising white-ball game for Kalyani Bengaluru Blasters.

Day 4 Highlights: Alexander Zverev beats Valentin Royer, Elena Rybakina into Round 3Wimbledon 2026 live score: Defending champions Iga Switak has beaten Karolina Pliskova to advance to the third round. Sixth seed Taylor Fritz has won his men's singles second-round match while Alexander Zverev has gone through to the third round.

Portugal vs Croatia livestreaming: Can Ronaldo deliver when it matters most?FIFA World Cup 2026: Portugal meet Croatia in Toronto in a high-stakes Round of 32 tie. The knockout contest could mark Cristiano Ronaldo's or Luka Modric's final appearance on football's biggest stage.

Galle, Colombo to host India in August Test series. Sri Lanka announces scheduleSri Lanka Cricket has confirmed Galle and Colombo as the venues for India's two-Test series in August. The matches carry World Test Championship importance for India as tougher assignments follow.

Shah Rukh Khan unveils new Los Angeles cricket stadium, thanks Jay Shah for supportShah Rukh Khan unveiled the Knight Riders Cricket Ground in Los Angeles, thanking Jay Shah and the ICC for support. The venue strengthens Major League Cricket in Southern California before cricket's LA28 Olympic return.

Sooryavanshi would have taken their pants off: Shastri's blunt verdict goes viralRavi Shastri has slammed India's decision to leave Vaibhav Sooryavanshi on the bench, insisting the 15-year-old should have debuted against Ireland and claiming he "would have taken their pants off" on a slow, small ground.

15-year-old made to wait: What exactly is India's plan for Vaibhav Sooryavanshi?India tour of England: Fast-tracked into the senior squad following a historic IPL campaign, the decision to leave 15-year-old prodigy Vaibhav Sooryavanshi on the bench invites a compelling question over India's delicate balance between hierarchy and current merit.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
